perturbation.original_text: 2.2. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, duplicate, transmit or otherwise disseminate in any manner the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, except to the Receiving Party’s officers, directors and employees (collectively “Employees”), and the Receiving Party’s agents, contractors and advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal advisors, auditors and accountants) retained by the Receiving Party (collectively “Advisors”).
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, duplicate, transmit or otherwise disseminate in any manner the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, except to the Receiving Party’s officers, directors and staff (collectively “Employees”), and the Receiving Party’s agents, contractors and consultants (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal advisors, auditors and accountants) retained by the Receiving Party (collectively “Advisors”).
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'advisors' to 'consultants' and 'employees' to 'staff' introduces uncertainty. It is unclear if consultants is equivalent to advisors. Also, Staff may not include Officers or Directors, who are included in the term Employees which creates further confussion. This limits who can receive the confidential information and creates an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2. The Receiving Party shall not disclose, duplicate, transmit or otherwise disseminate in any manner the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information, except to the Receiving Party’s officers, directors and staff (collectively “Employees”), and the Receiving Party’s agents, contractors and consultants (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal advisors, auditors and accountants) retained by the Receiving Party (collectively “Advisors”). All such persons receiving Confidential Information shall have a need to know and shall be bound by professional duty or in writing (in advance) to confidentiality MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT No Reservations Confidential Page 2 of 3 No Reservations (Mutual) NDA v01 and non-use obligations at least as protective of such information as this Agreement. The Receiving Party shall be liable to the Disclosing Party in the event that any of its Employees or Advisors breach such obligations, and upon the Disclosing Party’s request, the Receiving Party shall promptly provide documentary proof of its compliance with the immediately preceding sentence.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'advisors' to 'consultants' and 'employees' to 'staff'. The explanation says that this introduces uncertainty because it's unclear if 'consultants' is equivalent to 'advisors', and 'staff' may not include Officers or Directors. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which uses 'staff' instead of 'employees' and 'consultants' instead of 'advisors'. This limits who can receive the confidential information because staff may not include officers or directors and creates an in-text contradiction by altering the scope of who is included in Employees and Advisors. Thus, it's a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Confidential Information and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Proprietary Data and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase "Confidential Information" is replaced with "Proprietary Data". It is not clear whether 'Proprietary Data' is a subset of, or encompasses, 'Confidential Information,' which creates ambiguity in the ownership and rights associated with the data. The definition is not provided and the rest of the document refers to 'Confidential Information', not 'Proprietary Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Proprietary Data and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data' in section 3.1. The explanation states that it is unclear whether 'Proprietary Data' is a subset or encompasses 'Confidential Information,' creating ambiguity. The rest of the document refers to 'Confidential Information', not 'Proprietary Data'. Since the contradicted text also refers to 'Proprietary Data', this perpetuates the ambiguous use of a new, undefined term, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement the Receiving Party's right to use the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information ceases and the Receiving Party must, upon the Disclosing Party’s request and at the Receiving Party’s expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party or permanently destroy all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its power, possession or control.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement the Receiving Party's right to use the Disclosing Party’s Trade Secrets ceases and the Receiving Party must, upon the Disclosing Party’s request and at the Receiving Party’s expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party or permanently destroy all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its power, possession or control.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Trade Secrets' in the first part of the sentence introduces a contradiction. The Receiving Party's right to use 'Trade Secrets' ceases, but the obligation to return/destroy applies to 'Confidential Information'. It's now unclear if information that is confidential but not necessarily a trade secret needs to be returned or destroyed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3. Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement the Receiving Party's right to use the Disclosing Party’s Trade Secrets ceases and the Receiving Party must, upon the Disclosing Party’s request and at the Receiving Party’s expense, promptly return to the Disclosing Party or permanently destroy all of the Disclosing Party’s Confidential Information in its power, possession or control. Having acknowledged that it, particularly within a networked environment, the return and/or destruction of electronically stored information may be impossible, extremely or otherwise unduly difficult or costly, the immediately preceding sentence is not intended to, and does not obligate the Receiving Party to make extra-ordinary efforts to destroy electronically stored copies of the Confidential Information, provided that, in relation to any such copy that is not destroyed the Receiving Party’s obligations of confidentiality and restricted use shall continue as per the provisions of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 6.3 such that the Receiving Party's right to use 'Trade Secrets' ceases, while the obligation to return/destroy only applies to 'Confidential Information'. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, highlights the same issue. The change creates a contradiction because the obligation to return/destroy information is now narrower than the restriction on use. It's unclear if confidential information that isn't a trade secret needs to be returned/destroyed, leading to a clear and direct conflict within the document.
